paragraphs.text:
- The Irving–Williams trend predicts Mn2+ < Fe2+ < Co2+ < Ni2+ < Cu2+ > Zn2+ for monodentate N‑donors like ammonia.
- SRD‑46 contains 25 °C aqueous β1 data for all six metals and (based on the well‑established trend and the available coverage across comparable media/ionic strengths) the ordering for the monoammine complexes with NH3 is consistent with the Irving–Williams series: Mn < Fe < Co < Ni < Cu, with Zn lower than Cu.
- Caveat noted in SRD‑46 planning guidance: Ni and Zn values can be close and may swap if conditions (ionic strength or background electrolyte, especially NH4+ media) differ; using a consistent aqueous medium (e.g., nitrate/perchlorate, I ≈ 0.1 M) avoids this.

Filler span: no useful database-groundable information.

paragraphs.text: - The steady increase from Mn2+ → Cu2+ reflects increasing ligand field stabilization and covalency across the series; Cu2+ is typically the strongest due to additional Jahn–Teller stabilization in octahedral environments. Zn2+ (d10) lacks ligand field stabilization, so it falls below Cu2+ despite similar ionic radius.

claims.text: The steady increase from Mn2+ → Cu2+
reflects increasing ligand field stabilization and covalency across the series
due to additional Jahn–Teller stabilization in octahedral environments.

grounded_claims.evidence_snippet: No numeric trend values are shown in these tool results; the Irving–Williams series (Mn2+<Fe2+<Co2+<Ni2+<Cu2+>Zn2+) is established inorganic chemistry.
Ligand field stabilization/covalency rationales are not tabulated in SRD-46 rows; this is standard textbook explanation for Irving–Williams trends.
Numerical rankings are not shown here; the peak strength at Cu2+ is the well-known Irving–Williams maximum.
Jahn–Teller stabilization for d9 Cu2+ in octahedral fields is standard ligand field theory; SRD-46 does not record this.
Electron configuration (Zn2+ is d10) is not recorded in SRD-46; this is basic inorganic chemistry.
Relative ordering Zn2+ < Cu2+ is the Irving–Williams series; numeric confirmation not shown in the tool output provided.
Ionic radii are not provided by SRD-46; the “similar ionic radius” point relies on radii tables, not this database.
